scheme_name: State Medical Scholarship (Scholarship Scheme for MBBS & Allied Courses, Nursing and Paramedical Students)
page_title: State Medical Scholarship (Scholarship Scheme for MBBS & Allied Courses, Nursing and Paramedical Students)

details: The “State Medical Scholarship” is a scholarship scheme, launched by the Department of Health & Family Welfare, Government of Nagaland. Under the scheme, financial assistance is provided to students who are pursuing MBBS & Allied Courses, Nursing and Paramedical courses.
benefits: Under the scheme, financial assistance/scholarships will be provided to students according to the course/programme in which they are enrolled.
eligibility: A student should fulfill the following criteria to be eligible for this Scholarship:
The applicant/student should be an inhabitant of Nagaland.
Students selected through NEET/NSEE/State Departmental Examination only and undergoing medical, nursing and paramedical courses shall be eligible to apply.
Students failing to complete their curriculum within the specified duration of the course (repeaters) shall not be entitled to an additional stipend.

online: All eligible students shall apply through the Common Scholarship Portal:
To register, visit the official Common Scholarship Portal of Nagaland:

If the applicant is a new user, click on the "Register" option. Provide your MOBILE Number linked with AADHAAR to get an OTP and verify the mobile. Once Mobile is verified, provide essential details such as your name, email address and create a unique password. Click on the "Register" button to proceed.
Once your mobile number is verified, log in to your scholarship account using the credentials the applicant created during registration.
Step 03: Fill in the User Profile
Update your personal information, including name, date of birth, gender, category, Bank Account Details, etc.
Select your Scholarship Scheme, Institution, Institution State, Institution, District and proceed.
Provide details about your educational background, such as school/college name, course, year of study, etc.
Depending on the scholarship applicants are applying for, applicants will need to upload supporting documents. Commonly required documents include:
Carefully review all the information applicants have entered and the documents applicants have uploaded. Make sure everything is accurate and complete.
Once applicants are satisfied with your application, click on the "Submit" button.
After submitting your application, applicants can track its status on the portal:
Log in to your account and check for updates on your application's status regularly.
The Department will accept applications through the Portal (online) only.
Application submitted in Hardcopy to the Directorate will not be entertained and shall be considered as not applied.
Applications with Incomplete/Unclear documents will be summarily rejected.
Applications received after the last date of submission will be rejected.
On submission a unique Registration No will be generated. Applicants are required to note this number for Renewal in the subsequent years.
The Department will not be responsible for non-receipt of scholarship due to wrong/inactive bank account number/details provided by the applicant.

Applicants is warned that if he/she gives false statements/declarations/documents etc. or otherwise obtained a scholarship through fraudulent means, he/she will be blacklisted and debarred from getting a scholarship under this scheme or any other scholarship schemes for the entire period of his/her studies. The scholarship amount if already paid will also be recovered.
Incomplete/wrong entries in the e-Form or incomplete/incorrect upload of necessary documents will be subject to rejection. Correction/ rectification after submission will not be entertained.

documents_required: Forwarding letter from the concerned Institution
A copy of the Nomination letter (NSEE/NEET/State Departmental Exam)
A copy of the Aadhaar Card
A copy of the SBI Account Number (first page of the Bank passbook or statement clearly indicating Account Number and Account Holders Name)
All the documents should be Self-Attest. Submission of unattested documents will lead to rejection of the application.
Bank account number should be of that of the applicant only. Only Bank Accounts of the State Bank of India will be accepted.

faqs.question: What is the State Medical Scholarship?
faqs.answer: The State Medical Scholarship is a scheme initiated by the Department of Health & Family Welfare, Government of Nagaland to provide financial assistance to students pursuing MBBS & Allied Courses, Nursing, and Paramedical courses.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for the Scholarship?
faqs.answer: Applicants must be residents of Nagaland and should be enrolled in medical, nursing, or paramedical courses after being selected through NEET/NSEE/State Departmental Examination.

faqs.question: What courses are covered under the State Medical Scholarship?
faqs.answer: The scholarship covers MBBS & Allied Courses, Nursing, and Paramedical courses.

faqs.question: Are repeaters eligible for the scholarship?
faqs.answer: No, students failing to complete their curriculum within the specified duration of the course (repeaters) are not entitled to additional stipend.

faqs.question: What are the general instructions for applying?
faqs.answer: All documents should be self-attested, and applications must be submitted online through the designated portal. Hardcopy submissions will not be entertained.

faqs.question: Which bank's account number should be provided?
faqs.answer: Only Bank Accounts of State Bank of India will be accepted, and the account number should belong to the applicant.

faqs.question: What if the scholarship amount is not received due to incorrect bank details?
faqs.answer: The Department will not be responsible for non-receipt of the scholarship due to wrong or inactive bank account details provided by the applicant.

faqs.question: What are the consequences of providing false information?
faqs.answer: If an applicant gives false statements, declarations, or documents, they will be blacklisted and debarred from receiving any scholarship under this or any other scheme. The scholarship amount, if already paid, will also be recovered.

faqs.question: How to apply under the scheme?
faqs.answer: All eligible students shall apply through the Common Scholarship Portal: https://scholarship.nagaland.gov.in

faqs.question: What happens if applications are submitted after the deadline?
faqs.answer: Applications received after the last date of submission will be rejected.

faqs.question: Can applicants from other states apply for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: No, only residents of Nagaland are eligible to apply.

faqs.question: Are there any specific academic requirements for eligibility?
faqs.answer: Applicants must have been selected through NEET/NSEE/State Departmental Examination for their respective courses.

faqs.question: How can applicants check the status of their application?
faqs.answer: Applicants can track their application status through the designated online portal.
